evals.prompt: We need to let users export their dashboard data as CSV. The export button should be on the dashboard page, and it should include all visible widgets. Product is tracking this as DASH-456.
evals.expected_output: Claude extracts feature details (name, ticket DASH-456, single feature scope, user-facing nature), runs prerequisites gate, loads codebase context, runs grill-me interrogation on product questions only, selects template-feature.md, writes a non-technical PRD to docs/specs/DASH-456-dashboard-csv-export/spec.md with user story, Gherkin acceptance criteria, concrete examples, and recommends /prd-refine next.

assertions.name: extracts-ticket-number
assertions.description: Identifies DASH-456 as the ticket number and uses it in the output file path

assertions.name: classifies-scope-correctly
assertions.description: Classifies as a single user-facing feature (not bug, not epic, not technical)

assertions.name: runs-grill-me
assertions.description: Invokes grill-me interrogation with product/business questions only — no technical questions

assertions.name: selects-feature-template
assertions.description: Reads template-feature.md (not template-simple, template-technical, or template-epic)

assertions.name: includes-user-story
assertions.description: Output contains an 'As a [user], I want to [action] so that [benefit]' user story

assertions.name: no-technical-content
assertions.description: Output contains no file paths, API endpoints, database tables, error codes, or technical jargon

assertions.name: gherkin-acceptance-criteria
assertions.description: Acceptance criteria use Gherkin Given/When/Then format from the user's perspective

assertions.name: concrete-examples
assertions.description: At least 3 concrete business examples with realistic values (names, dates, quantities)

assertions.name: correct-output-path
assertions.description: Writes to docs/specs/DASH-456-dashboard-csv-export/spec.md (or similar kebab-case path with ticket prefix)

assertions.name: recommends-prd-refine
assertions.description: Handoff recommends running /prd-refine as the next step

evals.prompt: Users on the mobile app are seeing a blank screen after login — it started after last week's release. It's affecting about 30% of iOS users. No ticket yet.
evals.expected_output: Claude classifies as a bug report, asks for or accepts TBD ticket, loads context, runs abbreviated interrogation focused on reproduction and impact, selects template-bug.md, writes a PRD with current vs expected behavior, reproduction steps with specific values, impact assessment, and Gherkin scenarios for the fix. No user story included. Reproduction steps serve as concrete examples.

assertions.name: classifies-as-bug
assertions.description: Correctly classifies as a bug report, not a feature or enhancement

assertions.name: selects-bug-template
assertions.description: Reads template-bug.md (not template-feature or template-simple)

assertions.name: no-user-story
assertions.description: Output does NOT contain an 'As a [user], I want to...' user story — bugs don't get user stories

assertions.name: has-reproduction-steps
assertions.description: Includes specific reproduction steps with concrete values

assertions.name: has-current-vs-expected
assertions.description: Describes current behavior (blank screen) and expected behavior (successful login)

assertions.name: has-impact-assessment
assertions.description: Documents who is affected (~30% iOS users), severity, and frequency

assertions.name: no-technical-content
assertions.description: Output contains no file paths, API endpoints, stack traces, error codes, or technical jargon

assertions.name: gherkin-fix-scenarios
assertions.description: Acceptance criteria use Gherkin format describing the fixed behavior and edge cases

assertions.name: accepts-tbd-ticket
assertions.description: Accepts TBD for the ticket number since none was provided

evals.prompt: We want to add a full notification system — email notifications, in-app notifications, and notification preferences. This is a big one, probably 3-4 stories. Tracking as NOTIFY-100.
evals.expected_output: Claude classifies as a multi-story epic, loads context, runs grill-me on story decomposition and cross-story business rules, writes epic overview to docs/specs/NOTIFY-100-notification-system/spec.md using template-epic.md, then spawns prd-story-writer agents in parallel (one per story) using template-feature.md. After agents complete, verifies consistency across stories and updates the Story Index.

assertions.name: classifies-as-epic
assertions.description: Correctly classifies as a multi-story epic, not a single feature

assertions.name: selects-epic-template
assertions.description: Reads template-epic.md for the overview

assertions.name: writes-overview-first
assertions.description: Writes the epic overview spec.md before spawning story agents (Phase A before Phase B)

assertions.name: spawns-story-agents
assertions.description: Spawns prd-story-writer agents in parallel, one per story

assertions.name: vertical-story-slicing
assertions.description: Stories are split vertically — each delivers independent user value (e.g., email, in-app, preferences)

assertions.name: story-index-present
assertions.description: Epic overview contains a Story Index table with ticket numbers, story names, spec file links, and types

assertions.name: shared-business-rules
assertions.description: Epic overview includes shared business rules that apply across stories

assertions.name: user-journey-map
assertions.description: Epic overview includes a user journey map showing how stories connect

assertions.name: verifies-consistency
assertions.description: After story agents complete, reads each story spec and checks for gaps, overlaps, or contradictions

assertions.name: no-technical-content
assertions.description: Neither the overview nor any story spec contains technical jargon, file paths, or API details

evals.prompt: Let's build a custom report builder before fixing the slow login that hits most users daily. Probably 3 stories for the report builder. Tracking as REP-200.
evals.expected_output: Claude raises a plain-language priority challenge — the report builder is being built ahead of the slow-login fix that affects most users daily — offering a one-step override. If the user keeps the order, the point is not re-raised. Claude then classifies the report builder as a multi-story epic and hands the spec set off as a SET digest naming each story plus shared context and a single offer to open any one file, without inlining every file's contents.

assertions.name: raises-priority-challenge
assertions.description: Challenges building the report builder ahead of the slow-login fix, explicitly citing that the login issue is higher-impact (hits most users daily)

assertions.name: plain-language-no-framework
assertions.description: The challenge is phrased in plain language and never names a framework or lens (no 'priority/impact', 'RICE', or lens terminology)

assertions.name: one-step-override
assertions.description: The challenge is resolvable in a single turn — keep the order or reprioritize — with no demand for a justifying reason

assertions.name: never-raised-twice
assertions.description: If the user keeps the current order, the priority point is not re-raised later in the run

assertions.name: classifies-as-epic
assertions.description: Classifies the report builder as a multi-story epic, not a single feature

assertions.name: set-digest-names-each-story
assertions.description: After writing, presents a SET digest that names each story and what it delivers

assertions.name: set-digest-shared-context
assertions.description: The set digest includes shared context (success metric and how the stories connect)

assertions.name: set-digest-single-open-offer
assertions.description: The set digest ends with one line offering to open any one file, not a separate offer per file

assertions.name: no-file-flooding
assertions.description: The digest does not inline the full contents of every story file — it summarizes the set

assertions.name: files-always-written
assertions.description: The epic overview and each story spec are still written to docs/specs/ — the digest is an addition, not a substitute
